sections.paragraph:
(kindred to גָּזַז) pr. to cut in pieces; hence-

(1) to pass through, to pass over, or away, i.q. Arab. جَازَ Med. Waw, Syr. ܓܙܳ to pass away, to fail; Psa 90:10, בִּי־גָז חִישׁ וַנָּעֻפָה “for it (human life) soon passes away, and we fly away.”

(2) caus. to cause to pass away, to bring over; Num 11:31, “a wind went forth from Jehovah וַיָּגָֽז שַׁלְוִים מִן־הַיָּם and brought quails from the sea;” LXX. ἐξεπέρασεν, Vulg. detulit, the Hebrew interpreters, and cut off from the sea; compare גזז. As to the word גֹּוזִי Psa 71:6, see the root גָּזָה .
